channel.title: Portfolio
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: 2. Sensor Fusion using 3D Gaussian Splatting
item.description:
Gaussian Splatting for Object Detection using 4D Radar and Camera. In this work, we fused monocular camera data with 4D radar data in the feature space using a Gaussian representation. This was for the Advanced Machine Perception course.

Programme page in the TU Delft Studyguide
Final Assignment paper: Gaussian miniCaR

item.title: 3. Bachelor Thesis
item.description:
Comparison of Machine Learning Models for Generating Ship Movements. This includes analyzing the previously recorded data using drone controllers and processing them into learnable parameters. These predicted ship movements were later executed on a small movement platform that simulates the ship's movements in the lab (not part of the thesis). This project was done in cooperation with the Fraunhofer Institute for Manufacturing Technology and Advanced Materials IFAM in Bremen. Data processing Classical machine learning techniques

Fraunhofer IFAM in Bremen
Bachelor Thesis Gunnar Martens

item.title: 1. Digital Twins for Smarter Greenhouses
item.description:
(in Development)Designing an autonomous mobile solution (Mirte Master) for a group-based project (5 members) to monitor plant growth in a complex greenhouse environment. The system required high-precision mapping and safe navigation within narrow aisles and variable lighting conditions. Python Gazebo ROS2 Machine Perception (YOLO)

Programme page in the TU Delft Studyguide

item.title: 5. PLC Programming Student Job
item.description:
Programming of a manufacturing plant simulator consisting of a small gantry crane style as part of an industrial manufacturing demonstrator. This device will be used to teach university students to work with and debug industrial grade infrastructure. PLC programming TIA Portal ST (structured text) FUB, KOP, ... Digital Communication protocols MQTT Monitoring in NodeRed Creation of electrical and logic plans EPLAN

Project Page at the Hochschule Bremen (in German)

item.title: Portfolio
item.description: Designing an autonomous mobile solution (Mirte Master) for a group-based project (5 members) to monitor plant growth in a complex greenhouse environment. This project is still in development.Gaussian Splatting for Object Detection using 4D Radar and Camera.Comparison of Machine Learning Models for Generating Ship Movements.Modifying the Cobot system and extending the capabilities to make it use different endeffectors to spray a liquid on a predefined surface.Programming of a manufacturing plant simulator consisting of a small gantry crane style as part of an industrial manufacturing demonstrator. The final exam for this apprenticeship covers mechanical manufacturing, electrical wiring, PLC programming, system testing, and technical communication for mechatronics technicians.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Designing an autonomous mobile solution (Mirte Master) for a group-based project (5 members) to monitor plant growth in a complex greenhouse environment. This project is still in development.Gaussian Splatting for Object Detection using 4D Radar and Camera.Comparison of Machine Learning Models for Generating Ship Movements.Modifying the Cobot system and extending the capabilities to make it use different endeffectors to spray a liquid on a predefined surface.Programming of a manufacturing plant simulator consisting of a small gantry crane style as part of an industrial manufacturing demonstrator. The final exam for this apprenticeship covers mechanical manufacturing, electrical wiring, PLC programming, system testing, and technical communication for mechatronics technicians.

item.title: 4. Capability Extention of a Cobot
item.description:
Modifying the Cobot system and extending the capabilities to make it use different endeffectors to spray a liquid on a predefined surface. This involved designing mechanical parts, rewiring internal electronics, Designing mechanical parts Solidworks Adding sensors and actuators to the cobot Pneumatic valve Programming the robot Internal block-diagram programming language Connection of different subsystems
Hahn Robotics Diepenau (in German)

item.title: 6. Final Examination Mechatronics Apprenticeship
item.description:
Final Examination Part 2: Mechatronics (2020): The final exam for this apprenticeship covered mechanical manufacturing, electrical wiring, PLC programming, system testing, and technical communication for mechatronics technicians. Mechanical Manufacturing: Precision machining of components (drilling, countersinking, reaming) and assembly of complex assemblies according to technical drawings (e.g., fabrication of housing components). Electrical Engineering & Wiring: Setup and wiring of control cabinets according to circuit diagrams while adhering to VDE standards; integration of sensors and actuators (e.g., illuminated pushbuttons, limit switches). Automation Technology (PLC): Programming and optimization of control sequences; implementation of operating modes (manual/automatic) using GRAFCET and transfer to the target system (Beckhoff PLC). Commissioning & System Testing: Systematic functional testing of mechatronic subsystems, troubleshooting, and documentation of test results. Technical Communication: Explanation of technical relationships and process-related decisions in situational specialist discussions.
Apprenticeships at Harting
Mechatroniker in the German Qualifications Framework (in German)
Mechatroniker at the Bielefeld Chamber of Commerce and Industry (in German)
